page.title: Integrations
page.description: Manage MCP servers, feishu credentials, and autopilot-bot deployments.
page.empty: No integrations yet. Click New integration to add one.

config.heading: Config
config.must_be_object: Config must be a JSON object
config.switch_form_needs_object: Cannot switch to form: top-level value must be a JSON object.
config.switch_form_invalid_json: Cannot switch to form — fix JSON first: {{message}}

actions.delete_confirm: Delete this integration and all its secrets?

mcp.command_hint: The binary to spawn, e.g. node / python / path to executable
mcp.working_dir_hint: Optional. Defaults to the runtime process cwd.
mcp.arguments_hint: One CLI argument per row. e.g. ./mcp-server.js · --port · 9000
mcp.env_hint: Non-secret env. Secret API tokens go to the Secrets section below.

feishu.app_id_hint: From feishu.cn / lark.com developer console. e.g. cli_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
feishu.base_url_hint: Override the API host. Leave empty to use the default for the selected domain.
feishu.team_chat_id_hint: The OAuth-resolved chat the bot belongs to. e.g. oc_xxxxxxxxxxxxxx
feishu.tenant_key_hint: Optional. Set when integrating a multi-tenant store app.
feishu.webhook_url_hint: Incoming webhook URL for posting messages from automation. (Custom Bot URL.)
feishu.secrets_hint: Secrets: add FEISHU_APP_SECRET, FEISHU_VERIFICATION_TOKEN, and (if your app uses encrypted events) FEISHU_ENCRYPT_KEY in the Secrets section below. Never paste these into config.

autopilot.bot_name_hint: Display name shown in autopilot dashboards.
autopilot.schedule_hint: Cron expression in 5-field form (e.g. "0 9 * * 1" — every Monday 09:00) or "manual" for trigger-only.
autopilot.target_workspace_hint: Workspace the bot acts on. Leave empty to default to the workspace owning this integration.
autopilot.description_hint: One-line summary for the bot list.
autopilot.health_check_hint: Optional. multica pings this URL to confirm the bot's runtime is alive.
autopilot.secrets_hint: Secrets: any signing secrets (e.g. WEBHOOK_SIGNING_SECRET) or API tokens the bot needs go in the Secrets section below.
